description: The Japanese House of Councillors passed a rare censure motion against Prime Minister Yasuo Fukuda in 2008. While non-binding, it signaled deep legislative paralysis during a period of Japanese political instability.

key_events.description: Opposition parties unite to challenge the government's policy direction.
Public polling shows frustration with economic stagnation.
The House of Councillors passes the censure motion.
PM Fukuda ignores the motion, resulting in legislative stalemate.

calibration_notes: Highlights scenarios where symbolic legislative actions (censure) function as political theater rather than removal.
